description_long: This barn features a long, rectangular structure with a prominent, steeply pitched gable roof that extends over the entire length of the building. The roof is clad in dark, overlapping shingles, giving it a weather-resistant appearance. The exterior walls are constructed from stone or brickwork, arranged in a uniform horizontal pattern, providing a sturdy and rustic look. There are no visible doors or windows on the sides shown, suggesting a utilitarian design focused on storage or shelter. The overall form is simple and functional, with clean lines and minimal decorative elements. The barn's robust construction and traditional materials make it suitable for agricultural or storage purposes in an urban or rural setting.
description: A long, rectangular barn with a steep gable roof and stone or brick siding, featuring a simple, utilitarian design with no visible doors or windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the barn, highlighting the dark gable roof and stone or brick siding with no visible openings.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the narrow end of the barn, emphasizing the steep pitch of the roof and the solid stone or brick wall.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, again showing the length of the barn with the same roof and siding, and no visible doors or windows.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, identical to the first side, focusing on the roof's steep angle and the solid wall construction.
attribute_door_style: single large hinged door (implied, not visible)
